method: Static-character isolation via spectrally-neutral test signals. Delta = output measurement - input measurement. EQ shape is makeup-gain-normalized band energy. See reports/ and tools/.

notes: This is a measurement dataset only; no YES Master DSP or preset calibration constants were changed.
Spatial is included because it exists in the current Preset enum/dsp.rs even though the downloaded prompt's older example list omitted it.
Custom is rendered as YES Master's neutral Custom calibration (Preset::Custom), not a saved user preset.

protocol.constant_across: all 8 signals x current YES Master preset list
protocol.input_gain_policy: Fixed at 0 dB. Source analysis is still injected because that is how the desktop app resolves adaptive guardrails.
protocol.output_format: WAV, source sample rate and source bit depth for the test-signal corpus

pink_noise_minus20.wav.purpose: Primary tonal reference. Equal energy per octave; output delta IS the EQ curve.

pink_noise_minus14.wav.purpose: Level-dependent check. Spectral delta vs -20 reveals level-dependent processing.

pink_noise_minus10.wav.purpose: Hot-level behavior. Pushes compressor/limiter; reveals stand-down on loud material.

sine_sweep_minus20.wav.purpose: Precise frequency response. Reveals narrow-band features broadband noise misses.

click_track.wav.purpose: Compressor timing. Attack, release, transient shaping.

tone_ladder_minus20.wav.purpose: Discrete per-frequency gain. Exact dB at known frequencies.

dynamic_test_minus14.wav.purpose: Dynamic range preservation. Maintains 20 dB contrast or crushes it?

mid_side_test_minus20.wav.purpose: Stereo width. Narrowing, widening, or rebalancing?

stereo.note: positive width_change = wider; correlation up = narrower

click_response.note: Sparse 1-sample clicks every 500 ms CANNOT measure limiter release. The raw transient_peak/release values (reshaping_raw) are confounded by per-preset click WIDENING through the 1 ms envelope, not limiter aggressiveness (adversarially verified). Honest click metrics: output_true_peak (do clicks survive to clip?) and loudness_lift (how hard the preset pulls up sparse transients). Real limiter timing needs a dense-transient source (drum loop / shaped bursts).

level_dependence.note: Values falling as the input gets louder => adaptive loudness-targeting. makeup_gain is an RMS delta; loudness_lift is a LUFS delta (the honest 'chase'). They diverge when a preset reshapes the spectrum (RMS can fall while LUFS rises).

stereo.note: positive width_change = wider; correlation up = narrower
